title: Gatefold pages in journals
subtitle: Inserting folded pages that open wider than the journal format, for maps, timelines, and panoramic spreads
excerpt: A gatefold is a page wider than the journal's own page width, folded in so it sits flush when closed and opens out to a larger working surface. Tipped into a bound journal on one narrow edge, a gatefold can double or triple the available spread width without altering the binding. Maps, family trees, timelines, and panoramic drawings are natural uses.
sourceNotes: General bookbinding and bookarts tradition. Gatefold construction is a standard insert technique documented in bookbinding manuals from the 19th century onwards.

glossaryTerms.definition: A folded insert wider than the host journal's page format, which unfolds to reveal a larger surface. The term comes from print production, where two parallel folds create panels that open like a gate.
A page or insert adhered into a bound journal along one narrow edge, typically a 5-8 mm glue strip, so that it opens like a normal page while lying flat. The glue edge is hidden inside the binding.

A narrow strip left unprinted or unworked at the binding edge of an insert, typically 5-8 mm. The hinge allowance is the glue surface; if the insert is adhered all the way to its edge, the fold is rigid and the insert will not open flat.

content.text: insert gives an A5 journal an occasional A4 spread, or an A4 journal an A3 panorama. The insert is prepared separately, then
beside the page it should accompany. Because only one narrow edge is glued, the rest of the insert opens freely.
Measure the journal's page width (W) and height (H). For a single gatefold that opens to double width, cut the insert to (2W + 8 mm) × H. The extra 8 mm is the
Score a fold line 8 mm from the left edge using the scoring tool and ruler. Score again at (W + 8 mm) from the left edge. These two score lines define the hinge and the fold respectively.

Fold the 8 mm hinge strip inward (away from the working face) along the first score line. Fold the main body of the insert along the second score line so that it closes flush against the hinge strip. Run the bone folder firmly along both folds.

Apply a thin, even line of acid-free PVA to the outer face of the 8 mm hinge strip only, do not let adhesive stray onto the rest of the insert. Press the hinge strip firmly against the journal page at the intended position and hold with a bone folder for 60 seconds. Leave under a light weight for 30 minutes before opening.

items.symptom: The gatefold does not open flat, it stays kinked at the fold
items.cause: The fold line was not scored before folding, so the paper is fighting the fold rather than following a weakened line.
items.fix: Score all fold lines before making any folds. On 130 gsm paper, a single firm pass with the scoring tool against a metal ruler is sufficient.

items.symptom: The insert falls out of the journal after a few openings
items.cause: Too little PVA on the hinge strip, or the PVA was dry before pressing.
items.fix: Apply a slightly thicker line of PVA and press immediately. Hold with a clip or weight for at least 30 minutes. On very smooth journal paper, lightly roughen the glue surface with sandpaper before applying PVA.
